steps.title: Spot the key requirement
steps.content: The task asks to summarize the kelp highway hypothesis — not just name it or describe supporting details. A summary of a theory must state what the theory claims, including its central mechanism, not just its context or consequences.

steps.title: Apply the requirement to the choices
steps.content:
Choice C (‘Humans may have first arrived in the Americas by sea, traveling between Pacific Ocean islands and coastlines and subsisting on a variety of resources.’) states the complete hypothesis: humans arrived by sea, traveled between Pacific islands and coastlines, and subsisted on available resources.

This is the only choice that includes all three components of the claim.

The other choices either focus on one supporting detail, merely name the hypothesis or describe what happened to the landscape afterward.

That confirms C is correct.
